description: This figure comprises a high-resolution TEM lattice image and associated diffraction patterns for aged 3Cu steel (Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu) after aging at 550 °C for 3 h. The main HRTEM micrograph shows nanoscale lattice fringes of the austenitic matrix (γ) containing co-precipitated κ-carbide (marked γ+κ, yellow dashed box) and Cu-rich phase (marked γ+Cu, red solid box), with a 5 nm scale bar. The yellow-bordered inset diffraction pattern along zone axis Z=[011] exhibits reflections including the (011) superlattice spot and (111), consistent with the ordered L′1₂ structure of κ-carbide. The red-bordered inset pattern along Z=[011] displays spots indexed as (111) and (200), which are characteristic of an FCC crystal structure for the Cu-rich phase. Collectively, these patterns confirm that both precipitates maintain coherency with the austenitic matrix, supporting a cube-on-cube orientation relationship. Although the caption identifies the insets as SAED patterns and a citation sentence reports BCC structure for the Cu-rich phase from SAED analysis, the visibly indexed reflections in the provided red-box pattern correspond to FCC diffraction geometry.
